message:
fcp-protocol: use checked_add in FCPC decode length arithmetic

Audit of binary-framing decode paths in crates/fcp-protocol surfaced
one concrete arithmetic-robustness gap; the rest of the surface is
already well-guarded and is documented as clean in this commit.

[LOW] FCPC `decode_with_limit` sums unchecked

  In `FcpcFrame::decode_with_limit`:

      let expected_len = FCPC_HEADER_LEN + claimed + FCPC_TAG_LEN;

  `claimed` is `header.len as usize`, bounded only by
  `max_payload_len`. On 32-bit targets (`usize == u32`) this wraps
  when a caller passes a large `max_payload_len` (e.g. `usize::MAX`)
  and the attacker sets `header.len = u32::MAX`: `36 + u32::MAX + 16`
  wraps to a tiny number, `bytes.len() != expected_len` may
  spuriously succeed, and the subsequent `bytes[cipher_start..cipher_end]`
  panics (DoS). 64-bit targets are safe in practice since `u32::MAX
  + 52` fits comfortably in `usize`.

  Fix: use `checked_add` with early return to `PayloadTooLarge` on
  overflow. Fail-closed. Regression test
  `decode_with_limit_rejects_overflowing_claim` drives a header with
  `len = u32::MAX` through `decode_with_limit(bytes, usize::MAX)`
  and asserts the returned error is `LengthMismatch` (64-bit path,
  arithmetic succeeded) or `PayloadTooLarge` (32-bit path, checked_add
  caught overflow), never a panic or silent accept.

Clean findings (no patch needed):

- FCPS (`FcpsFrameHeader::decode`, `FcpsFrame::decode`): length
  validation goes through `validate_frame_lengths` which already uses
  `checked_add`/`checked_mul` for every derived length. The
  post-validation `Vec::with_capacity(header.symbol_count as usize)`
  allocation is bounded by `max_datagram_bytes / record_size`
  because `validate_frame_lengths` has proven
  `symbol_count * record_size == total_payload_len ==
  bytes.len() - FCPS_HEADER_LEN <= max_datagram_bytes`.

- Signed FCPS frames (`SignedFcpsFrame::decode`): explicit MIN_LEN
  check + per-field bounds check before slicing; `source_id_len` is
  u16 so `2 + source_id_len + ...` can't overflow usize on 32-bit.

- Session CBOR (`decode_canonical_cbor`): payload size capped by
  `MAX_HANDSHAKE_BYTES` before `ciborium::from_reader`, trailing-byte
  check prevents truncation bypass, canonical re-encode check blocks
  non-deterministic CBOR. Boundary is tight.

- SymbolRecord / SymbolRecordRef decode: `bytes.len() < expected_len`
  guard covers all subsequent slices; `SYMBOL_RECORD_OVERHEAD (22) +
  symbol_size (u16 max 65535)` cannot overflow usize on any target.

- Truncated-frame handling: every decode returns `TooShort` with the
  observed and minimum-expected lengths rather than panicking on
  out-of-bounds slices.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
